LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED ON FEB-21-2023, THE COURT NOW GRANTS DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY OF PROCEEDINGS. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. (302/RBU)
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 210042115) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FARR, SIEARA
OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION (TRANSACTION ID # 210043309) FILED BY DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE MOTION IS TIMELY AND JURISDICTIONAL, IT DOES NOT REGARD A "CHANGE IN LAW" AS PLAINTIFF ASSERTS. (SEE CCP 1008(C).) RATHER, COURTS OF APPEAL ARE SPLIT ON THE RELEVANT LAW. ON JULY 19, 2023, THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT GRANTED A PETITION FOR REVIEW TO RESOLVE THE SPLIT. THIS COURT DEFERS TO OUR HIGH COURT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-11-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-02-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendant Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Appearances via Zoom: Maite Colon of the Knight Law Group, LLP (310.552.2250) for plaintiff and moving party; Oliver Smith of Lewis Brisbois (415.362.2580) for defendant and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration is denied.

Assuming without deciding that the motion is timely and jurisdictional, it does not regard a "change in law" as plaintiff asserts. (See CCP 1008(c).) Rather, courts of appeal are split on the relevant law. On July 19, 2023, the California Supreme Court granted a petition for review to resolve the split. This court defers to our high court.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Timothy Lupiner, Esq. 310-552-2250 Knight Law Group for Plaintiff.
Oliver Smith, Esq. 415-438-5930 Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant.
Court reporter: Gina Gomez, CSR #12775, gg.1csr@gmail.com, 858-735-7955, reported.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Of Proceedings

Taken Under Submission. Both sides to submit a proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Gina Gomez, CSR #12775, gg.1csr@gmail.com, 858-735-7955, reported. (302/rbu)
